metadata.summary: Bundle of four methods for solopreneur idea discovery: 7P framework, Paul Graham questions, pain-point mining, 5-dimension scoring.

content.title: Idea Generation Methods
content.summary: A bundle of four complementary pre-validation methods for solopreneur idea discovery: the 7P framework (Pain/Passion/Profession/Process/Platform/People/Product), Paul Graham's four diagnostic questions, systematic pain-point mining (complaint audit + workaround inventory), and a 5-dimension niche scoring matrix. Output: 3 prioritized candidate niches for downstream validation.

why.p: Self-generated ideas without structure over-index on personal bias, generic startup tropes, and currently trending topics. The 7P + PG + pain-audit sequence forces divergence from multiple angles; the scoring matrix filters to highest-signal candidates without hallucinated market data. Running all four in sequence takes more time but produces substantially less mode-collapsed output than a single brainstorm.

list.item: Earliest exploratory stage: founder with no concrete product target.
After a project shutdown or pivot — regenerating a fresh idea pool grounded in current pains.
Periodic side-project ideation: refilling the funnel (idea → niche-evaluation → problem-validation).
Structured diverge phase before using faion-brainstorm skill.
After committing to a niche — switch to feature-discovery and problem-validation.
For agency/consulting work where the idea is set by the client.
For incremental optimization on an existing product — wrong scope.
When founder cannot dedicate a week to ideation and follow-up; one-shot brainstorms decay.

section.name: 7p-framework
section.p: Seven lenses for generating candidate ideas. For each P, generate 3-5 ideas and score them on Market size (1-5) + Personal fit (1-5) + Urgency (1-5). Top 3 scores proceed to niche scoring.

list.item: Pain: What frustrates you daily? List 5 annoyances, rate severity 1-10.
Passion: What would you do for free? Activities and hobbies with sustainable energy.
Profession: What is broken in your industry? Insider knowledge of inefficiencies.
Process: What workflow takes 10x longer than it should?
Platform: What is missing from an existing platform? Missing integrations, poor UX.
People: What do your network contacts complain about repeatedly?
Product: What product would you pay for immediately if it existed?

section.name: paul-graham-questions
section.p: Four diagnostic questions for surfacing hidden opportunities:

list.item: What is tedious but necessary? High-frequency, low-satisfaction tasks everyone must do.
What is surprisingly hard to do? Things that should be simple but require workarounds.
What do you find yourself building for yourself? Personal tools that could scale.
What would you pay for that does not exist? Direct willingness-to-pay signal.

rule.statement: Time-box each PG question to 30 minutes of stream-of-consciousness writing. No editing during generation; convergence comes in the scoring phase.
rule.rationale: Editing during ideation activates the critic and suppresses unconventional candidates. Unfiltered output then convergence produces broader coverage than filtered ideation.

section.name: pain-point-mining
section.p: Four techniques for surfacing normalized pains you may have stopped noticing:

list.item: Complaint audit: log every complaint for one week, note frequency and intensity, categorize by work/personal/tools.
Workaround inventory: list spreadsheets, scripts, and processes you maintain to work around broken tools.
Tool stack analysis: list all tools you use, rate satisfaction 1-5, identify inter-tool gaps.
Time tracker: track where time goes for 3 days, identify recurring time sinks, calculate cost of inefficiency.

section.p: Scoring weights for pain mining output: Frequency (30%), Intensity (30%), Current solutions (20%), Your ability to solve (20%).

section.name: niche-scoring
section.p: Score each idea 1-5 on five dimensions (max 25 total):

list.item: Market Size: 1-2 = less than $10M, 3-4 = $10M-$100M, 5 = greater than $100M
Competition: 1-2 = red ocean 10+ competitors, 3-4 = moderate 3-10, 5 = blue ocean less than 3
Barriers: 1-2 = high (capital, regulatory), 3-4 = medium, 5 = low (technical only)
Monetization: 1-2 = unclear, 3-4 = possible, 5 = obvious path
Personal Fit: 1-2 = no relevant skills, 3-4 = some skills, 5 = perfect match

rule.statement: Any score of 4 or 5 on Market Size or Competition requires a cited source URL or a "missing_data" flag. Do not accept unsupported high scores.
rule.rationale: These two dimensions are the highest fabrication-risk dimensions for LLM agents. Without a citation requirement, agents assign 4-5 to any idea they generated, producing systematically inflated totals.

rule.statement: Score ideas in batches of 10 or more. Absolute scores on single ideas are meaningless without comparison context.
rule.rationale: Isolated scores have no calibration reference. A score of 18/25 is only meaningful when compared against other candidates from the same batch.

rule.statement: Run the four methods in sequence: (1) divergent ideation via 7P + PG, (2) pain audit to refine candidates, (3) niche scoring to filter top 3. Do not run all in parallel — they build on each other.
rule.rationale: Running all methods simultaneously generates overlapping, unsequenced output that is harder to deduplicate and filter. Sequential execution each feeds refined input to the next stage.

antipattern.description: LLMs default to "AI scheduler", "task tracker", "AI for X" outputs under default temperature settings.
antipattern.reason: Training data for solopreneur ideation is dominated by these patterns. Without explicit diversity forcing, agents repeat them across every framework and every session.

rule.statement: Set temperature to 0.8-0.9 for divergent ideation agents. Forbid "AI-powered", "smart", "intelligent", "GPT" as name prefixes in the prompt to force diverse framing.
rule.rationale: These terms are statistical attractors. Explicitly prohibiting them in the prompt forces the model to find alternative framings that expose different parts of the idea space.

antipattern.description: Agents conflate "passion" ideas with "profession" ideas, producing near-duplicate candidates across two P categories.
antipattern.reason: Without a strict definition prompt-side, the model treats passion and profession as semantically overlapping domains.

antipattern.description: PG question 3 ("what do you find yourself building for yourself") produces Dropbox/Notion clones without founder-specific personal context.
antipattern.reason: The model defaults to well-known successful answers from training data when personal context is absent.

rule.statement: Provide concrete founder context before running PG question 3: tools actually built, workflows automated, scripts written in the past 6 months. Generic profile → generic output.
rule.rationale: This question requires specific personal context to produce non-generic results. Without it, the agent falls back to pattern-matching against training data success stories.

list.item: Checkpoint 1: Founder profile accuracy before running ideation — verify skills, constraints, and daily routine are correctly captured.
Checkpoint 2: Shortlist review after generation, before scoring — founder reduces 35 ideas to 10-15 by gut before scoring begins.
Checkpoint 3: Top-3 sanity check before proceeding to niche-evaluation — founder confirms the three candidates are genuinely interesting, not agent-rationalized.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: This methodology covers four sub-methods (7P, paul-graham-questions, pain-point-research, niche-evaluation) bundled in one pipeline. Drive them as a sequenced pipeline: (1) divergent ideation across 7P + PG questions, (2) pain audit refines candidates, (3) niche scoring filters to top 3. Each step uses structured-output JSON. Final output is 3 candidate niches with research/validation plan.

list.item: divergent-ideator (sonnet, high-temperature) — runs 7P framework and PG questions per founder profile.
pain-miner (haiku) — extracts complaints/workarounds/tool-stack gaps from a journal or chat history corpus.
convergence-scorer (sonnet) — applies the 5-dimension scorecard (market/competition/barriers/monetization/fit).
faion-brainstorm skill — already exists in the workspace and pairs perfectly: diverge/converge/review cycles.
Run divergent ideation BEFORE looking at trending lists — external trends bias output toward "AI for X" tropes.
Time-box: 30 minutes per PG question, no editing during. Convergence comes later.
Pair self-generated pain audit with at least 5 cold conversations; otherwise you're solving for yourself only.
Score ideas in batches of 10+; absolute scores are meaningless without comparison context.
Kill ideas aggressively — keep top 3 per cycle. The methodology is filtration, not collection.
Re-run quarterly; markets move and your skill set evolves, so the input changes.

template.purpose: Per-P idea capture and scoring rows, top-3 section.
5-dimension scoring matrix with decision band table.
Divergent-ideation agent prompt (7P + PG, JSON output, high-temperature instructions).
Convergence-scorer agent prompt with missing-data flag requirement.
